perturbation.original_text: That, prior to finalizing an agreement to purchase a busine ss, it is my responsibility to make an independent verification of all information. I agree that Tameracq Partners, Inc is not responsible for the accuracy of any Informati on I received and I agree to indemnify and hold Tameracq Partners, Inc harmless from any claims or damages resulting from its use. I will look only to Seller and to my own investigation for all information regarding any business offered by the Franchisee.
perturbation.changed_text:
That all correspondence, inquiries, offers to purchase and negotiations relating to the purchase or lease or any business presented to me, or companies I represent, by the Blockbuster Franchisee, will be conducted excl usively through Tameracq Partners, Inc.

That, prior to finalizing an agreement to purchase a busine ss, it is my responsibility to make an independent verification of all information. However, Tameracq Partners, Inc guarantees the accuracy of all Information I received and I do not need to indemnify and hold Tameracq Partners, Inc harmless from any claims or damages resulting from its use. I am not required to look only to Seller and to my own investigation for all information regarding any business offered by the Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates the investor's responsibility for independent verification and indemnification. The modified text, placed in the 'Correspondence' section, removes the indemnification clause and contradicts the investor's responsibility, placing liability on Tameracq Partners, Inc which is not compliant with standard liability practices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission
perturbation.law_explanation: Misleading consumers by issuing guarantees without reasonable basis and removing responsibility of independent verification violates consumer protection laws. Specifically, advertising a guarantee of accuracy when no such guarantee exists and disclaiming liability contradicts the principle of fair competition and honest representation to potential investors.

perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly contradicts the original agreement by having Tameracq Partners, Inc guarantee the accuracy of all information and removing the investor's responsibility to indemnify them. This contradicts fair practice because, as the Law Explanation states, it issues guarantees without reasonable basis and removes the responsibility of independent verification. 15 USC 45 states that "Unfair methods of competition [are] unlawful," and advertising a guarantee of accuracy when no such guarantee exists is a violation of consumer protection laws.

perturbation.original_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to pur chase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Seller the right to obtain, through standard reporting agencies, financial and credit information concerning my self or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this informa tion will be held conf idential by Seller and Tameracq Partners, Inc and will only be used for the purpose of Seller extending credit to me.
perturbation.changed_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to pur chase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Seller the right to obtain, through standard reporting agencies, financial and credit information concerning my self or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this informa tion will be publicly disclosed by Seller and Tameracq Partners, Inc and will be used for any purpose they choose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be held confidential' to 'will be publicly disclosed' and 'only be used for the purpose of Seller extending credit to me' to 'will be used for any purpose they choose' directly contradicts standard privacy practices and fiduciary duties. This creates significant financial and legal risk for the investor.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 1681b - Conditions for furnishing consumer reports
perturbation.law_explanation: The Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) outlines permissible purposes for obtaining credit reports and mandates confidentiality. Allowing the seller to publicly disclose and use the information for any purpose violates FCRA's restrictions on data use and confidentiality.

perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts standard privacy practices by stating that financial and credit information 'will be publicly disclosed' and 'will be used for any purpose they choose,' whereas the original text ensures confidentiality and limits the use of information to extending credit. The Law Explanation supports this, stating the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) outlines permissible purposes for obtaining credit reports and mandates confidentiality, which is a direct violation of FCRA's restrictions. While the scraped snippets focus on amendments and effective dates, the core principle of protecting consumer financial information is clearly violated by the altered contract language allowing public disclosure and unrestricted use of the data.

perturbation.original_text: That information provided on businesses by the Blockbuster Franchisee is sensi tive and confidential and that its disclosure to others would be damaging to the businesses and to the Broker’s fiduciary relationship with the Seller. That I will not disclose any Information re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: That information provided on businesses by the Blockbuster Franchisee is publicly available and not confidential and that its disclosure to others would not be damaging to the businesses and to the Broker’s fiduciary relationship with the Seller. That I will disclose Information regarding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, and I do not need to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I do not agree to obtain their consent to maintain such confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'sensitive and confidential' to 'publicly available and not confidential' and reversing the non-disclosure agreement creates a contradiction. It directly conflicts with the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. Modifying it this way would allow the investor to disclose sensitive information, which hurts the seller and Broker’s fiduciary relationship with the seller.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Civil Code § 1644 - Sense of words
perturbation.law_explanation: This section relates to how contracts are interpreted, but the key point here is that terms are interpreted in their ordinary and popular sense. By changing the agreement to remove the obligation to not disclose information, the main intention of the contract is broken. California law requires that contractual terms be interpreted according to their ordinary meaning and the intention of the parties. It creates uncertainty in the agreement because the parties will be able to disclose confidential information at will.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1644. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1644. The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, rather than according to their strict legal meaning; unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or unless a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter must be followed. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: ON OF CONTRACTS - Sections 1635-1663 :: California Civil Code :: 2005 California Code :: California Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of the California Code 2024 California Code 2023 California Code 2022 California Code 2005 California Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2005 California Civil Code Sections 1635-1663 TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS CIVIL CODE SECTION 1635-1663 1635. All contracts, whether public or private, are to be interpreted by the same rules, except as otherwise provided by this Code. 1636. A contract must be so interpreted as to give effect to the mutual intention of the parties as it existed at the time of contracting, so far as the same is ascertainable and lawful. 1637. For the purpose of ascertaining the intention of the parties to a contract, if otherwise doubtful, the rules given in this Chapter are to be applied. 1638. The language of a contract is to govern its interpretation, if the language is clear and explicit, and does not involve an absurdity. 1639. When a contract is reduced to writing, the intention of the parties is to be ascertained from the writing alone, if possible; subject, however, to the other provisions of this Title. 1640. When, through fraud, mistake, or accident, a written contract fails to express the real intention of the parties, such intention is to be regarded, and the erroneous parts of the writing disregarded. 1641. The whole of a contract is to be taken together, so as to give effect to every part, if reasonably practicable, each clause helping to interpret the other. 1642. Several contracts relating to the same matters, between the same parties, and made as parts of substantially one transaction, are to be taken together. 1643. A contract must receive such an interpretation as will make it lawful, operative, definite, reasonable, and capable of being carried into effect, if it can be done without violating the intention of the parties. 1644. The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, rather than according to their strict legal meaning; unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or unless a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter must be followed. 1645. Technical words are to be interpreted as usually understood by persons in the profession or business to which they relate, unless clearly used in a different sense. 1646. A contract is to be interpreted according to the law and usage of the place where it is to be performed; or, if it does not indicate a place of performance, according to the law and usage of the place where it is made. 1646.5. Notwithstanding Section 1646, the parties to any contract, agreement, or undertaking, contingent or otherwise, relating to a transaction involving in the aggregate not less than two hundred fifty thousand dollars ($250,000), including a transaction otherwise covered by subdivision (1) of Section 1105 of the Commercial Code, may agree that the law of this state shall govern their rights and duties in whole or in part, whether or not the contract, agreement, or undertaking or transaction bears a reasonable relation to this state. This section does not apply to any contract, agreement, or undertaking (a) for labor or personal services, (b) relating to any transaction primarily for personal, family, or household purposes, or (c) to the extent provided to the contrary in subdivision (2) of Section 1105 of the Commercial Code. This section applies to contracts, agreements, and undertakings entered into before, on, or after its effective date; it shall be fully retroactive. Contracts, agreements, and undertakings selecting California law entered into before the effective date of this section shall be valid, enforceable, and effective as if this section had been in effect on the date they were entered into; and actions and proceedings commencing in a court of this state before the effective date of
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original contract's intention to maintain confidentiality, thus creating a legal contradiction. The original text emphasizes the sensitivity and confidentiality of the business information, while the changed text states it is 'publicly available and not confidential.' This directly opposes the nature of a non-disclosure agreement and fiduciary duty. The Law Explanation generated by the LLM states that California law requires that contractual terms be interpreted according to their ordinary meaning and the intention of the parties. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2, specifically California Civil Code Section 1644, reinforce this, stating that 'The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense.' The changed text alters the fundamental nature of the agreement from protecting sensitive information to allowing its disclosure, undermining the ordinary and popular understanding of a confidentiality agreement and fiduciary duty, thus establishing a clear and direct contradiction.
